trials.reason: Book A introduces the core query-params.ts type/library rewrite before the FilterBar/debouncedFilter consumer changes that depend on it, whereas Book B leads with FilterBar using APIs not yet defined.

Book B puts query-params.ts (the store-to-plain-object migration that all the filter components' prop-type changes depend on) before its consumers, whereas Book A opens with FilterBar/UserFilter changes that presuppose that migration hasn't happened yet; B also keeps the FilterBar/UserFilter/ProjectFilter concern group less interrupted by unrelated sections than A does.

Book B puts query-params.ts (the defining module) immediately before all its consumers as one contiguous block and groups the FilterBar/UserFilter/ProjectFilter concern together, producing fewer concern-switches and a shorter definition-to-use gap for debouncedFilter than Book A, which opens with FilterBar/UserFilter before query-params.ts even exists in the story and scatters the two concerns across more locations.

metadata.note: single-family judge (Claude): self-preference caveat applies; generator/judge ids must differ where possible
